evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts` mixes domain billing flows (ledger queries, Stripe checkout, admin grant-credits mutation) with UI concerns (`toast`, `window.location.assign`) inside a generic `shared/hooks` location.

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useCredits.test.ts` mocks legacy/different module boundaries (`@/shared/lib/errorHandler`, `@/shared/lib/queryKeys`) while production code uses `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError` and `@/shared/lib/queryKeys/credits`, signaling unstable package contracts.
`src/shared/lib/autoplayMonitor.ts` performs import-time dev behavior (auto-start `setTimeout` + `setInterval`) from `shared/lib`, blurring pure utility vs runtime bootstrap responsibilities.

`src/shared/lib/aspectRatios.ts` combines canonical ratio constants with UI preview/render helpers and retains an intentionally-unused internal function (`void getDisplayDimensions`), indicating boundary mixing within a foundational shared module.
Three near-pass-through hooks (`useCompletedTimestamp`, `useProcessingTimestamp`, `useUpdatingTimestamp`) mostly remap option names into `useRelativeTimestamp` with minimal behavior added.

Task-creation modules carry very large parameter interfaces and reconstruction layers (`JoinClipsTaskParams`, `IndividualTravelSegmentParams`, `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParamsInput` payload assembly) with substantial field forwarding and defaulting rather than domain-narrow abstractions.

Orchestration identifiers are duplicated across surfaces (`orchestration_contract`, nested `orchestrator_details`, top-level `run_id`/`orchestrator_task_id` variants), indicating abstraction leakage instead of one authoritative contract shape.

Orchestrator-subtask JSON path contract is duplicated across modules: `applyParentTaskFilter` hardcodes five null checks in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` lines 45-49, while `buildSubTaskFilter`/`extractOrchestratorRef` hardcode the same path family in `supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts` lines 45-50 and 87-94.

Scheduled invalidation lifecycle applies delay twice: `scheduleDedupedInvalidation` waits `delayMs` before calling `runInvalidationLifecycle` (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts` lines 86-89), and `runInvalidationLifecycle` waits `delayMs` again (lines 143-146).

`preloadGenerationTaskMappings` advertises optional full task preloading but does not await `queryClient.prefetchQuery` calls (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts` lines 49-63), so downstream consumers can observe partially preloaded state from the same API call.

Edge helper response contracts are inconsistent: `parseJsonBodyStrict` emits plain-text responses via `toErrorResponse` (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts` lines 73-75, 161-163) while `rateLimitResponse` emits JSON + CORS headers (`supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts` lines 216-230), creating non-uniform boundary behavior for sibling edge endpoints.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelinePositions.ts has high-comment-density defensive orchestration around refs/locks/timers (e.g., lines 157-213, 260-267, 394-397, 514-531), signaling brittle local control flow.
supabase/functions/update-task-status/request.ts repeatedly flushes and returns near-identical validation error branches (lines 40-109), creating verbose branch duplication.
supabase/functions/ai-prompt/index.ts uses long inline string assembly and multi-branch task handling in one file (lines 59-292), reducing local readability and increasing mutation risk.
supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts uses broad unchecked casts from unknown object fields (lines 46-50), reducing interface honesty and pushing validation burden to downstream branches.
supabase/functions/_shared/storagePaths.ts declares it is a direct copy of another runtime file and requires manual sync (lines 4-5), creating migration residue by design.
src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts is explicitly a legacy compatibility re-export shim (lines 2-6) instead of a removed migration layer.

supabase/functions/ai-prompt/index.ts contains very large repetitive instruction blocks with repeated 'CRITICAL' framing across tasks (lines 67-111, 205-251), consistent with LLM-style prompt bloat in production logic.

supabase/functions/_shared/systemLogger.ts and supabase/functions/ai-prompt/index.ts both use file-wide deno lint suppression (line 1 in each), which masks cleanup pressure on generated/boilerplate patterns.
Delayed generation invalidation applies `delayMs` in `setTimeout(...)` and then again in `runInvalidationLifecycle(...)`, creating an effective 2x delay path for scheduled invalidations.

`useAuthReferralFinalize` awaits `supabase.rpc(...)` but never inspects returned `{ error }`, so non-throwing RPC failures are silently treated as success and referral storage is always cleared in `finally`.

findings.summary: Domain type ownership is blurred by a reverse alias shim that routes domain code through a global `src/types` entrypoint.
evidence: `shot.ts` imports `GenerationRow` from `@/types/generationRow`.
`src/types/generationRow.ts` is only `export type { GenerationRow } from '@/domains/generation/types/generationRow';`, so the dependency direction is domain -> global alias -> domain.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical ownership path for generation domain types (prefer direct `@/domains/generation/types/*` imports in domain code), then keep `src/types/*` only as temporary compatibility adapters with a deprecation/removal plan.

findings.summary: Infrastructure lifecycle boundaries are ambiguous because explicit bootstrap is undermined by implicit lazy initialization.
evidence: `initializeSupabaseClient()` is documented as bootstrap-owned, but `getSupabaseClient()` always calls it, allowing hidden first-use initialization anywhere.
`buildSupabaseClient()` performs one-time instrumentation install and runtime initialization, making first caller location architecturally significant.
`handleError()` similarly mixes normalization/logging with notifier state checks and optional UI notification, concentrating multiple responsibilities in a globally imported helper.
findings.suggestion: Split service boot from service access: make initialization explicit and required at app startup, make getters fail fast if uninitialized in non-test contexts, and move notification/presentation policy out of core error normalization helpers.

findings.summary: Overlay interaction rules are owned by individual feature components instead of a shared interaction contract, increasing drift risk.
evidence: `tooltip.tsx` provides only low-level trigger/content primitives.
`CreativePartnerPane.tsx` implements custom mobile/desktop tooltip-open and disable toggling logic via `useEcosystemTooltipController`.
`InfoTooltip.tsx` implements separate controlled/uncontrolled open-state orchestration and conditional rendering policy.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a small shared overlay interaction adapter (for controlled/uncontrolled open state + mobile behavior policy) and have feature components provide only content-specific rules.

findings.summary: Multiple timestamp hooks are thin renaming wrappers over one primitive, adding indirection without meaningful behavioral boundaries.
evidence: Each hook forwards to `useRelativeTimestamp` with mostly renamed fields (`generationProcessedAt`/`generationStartedAt`/`date` -> `dateInput`) and fixed mode strings.
Only `useUpdatingTimestamp` adds a single default formatter, while still duplicating wrapper structure.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one exported hook with mode-specific option aliases (or explicit call sites using `useRelativeTimestamp`) and keep only wrappers that add real policy/validation.

findings.summary: Task payload builders expose oversized param bags and duplicate orchestration metadata across layers, weakening interface honesty.
evidence: `buildJoinClipsPayload` and `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` emit orchestration identity in multiple places (top-level and nested objects).
`checkOrchestratorCompletion` compensates by querying many possible JSON locations (`params->orchestration_contract`, `params->run_id`, `params->orchestrator_details->run_id`, etc.).
`IndividualTravelSegmentParams` and `JoinClipsTaskParams` include broad optional fields for multiple modes, then reconstruct normalized state via large fallback chains.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical orchestration envelope type shared by task creators and completion handlers, migrate producers to emit only that envelope, and remove fallback path probing once migrated.

findings.summary: Cross-module cache invalidation relies on mixed key conventions and raw key-shape assumptions, making integration seams brittle.
evidence: `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` invalidates some queries via explicit key factories and others via predicates on `query.queryKey[0]` string literals.
`DataFreshnessManager` depends on `JSON.stringify(queryKey)` identity and consumers manually pass keys from different key systems.
`useTasks` manually routes fetch success/failure into `dataFreshnessManager` using constructed keys, coupling query composition and freshness orchestration.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a typed query-key contract module for segment/task domains and refactor invalidation/freshness APIs to accept those typed keys only (no raw string-prefix predicates).

findings.summary: Timeline frame calculators perform direct index access on dragged arrays without local precondition guards.
evidence: `calculateDropAtPositionZero` immediately calls `pushUpdate(draggedShotGens[0].id, ...)` and later indexes by loop without checking non-empty alignment.
The algorithm assumes `draggedShotGens.length === draggedItemIds.length`, but this invariant is not asserted in the calculator itself.
findings.suggestion: Add explicit early-return/assert guards in calculator entry points for empty/mismatched dragged collections and surface a typed error or noop path.

findings.summary: Sub-task/orchestrator contract is hardcoded in multiple modules, risking drift across boundaries.
evidence: `applyParentTaskFilter` encodes orchestrator paths in frontend query helpers (`params->orchestration_contract->orchestrator_task_id`, `params->orchestrator_task_id_ref`, etc.) in `generationTaskRepository.ts` lines 45-49.
`buildSubTaskFilter` and `extractOrchestratorRef` encode the same contract independently in edge billing utilities (`billing.ts` lines 45-50 and 87-94).
Any schema/path evolution now requires synchronized edits across client and edge codepaths, with no shared contract source.
findings.suggestion: Define one shared orchestrator-reference schema helper (single exported list of JSON paths + extractor/filter builders) and consume it from both repository and edge billing modules to eliminate path drift.

findings.summary: Delayed generation invalidation applies delay twice, making lifecycle timing unpredictable.
evidence: `scheduleDedupedInvalidation` sets `setTimeout(..., delayMs)` before invoking `runInvalidationLifecycle` (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts` lines 86-89).
`runInvalidationLifecycle` then does `await wait(delayMs)` again (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts` lines 143-146).
Callers coordinating cache freshness/preload timing (for example adjacent-page prefetch + cleanup flows) receive invalidation later than requested, causing seam-level timing mismatch.
findings.suggestion: Apply delay in only one layer: either keep timer scheduling and remove delay handling from `runInvalidationLifecycle`, or pass zeroed options into lifecycle after scheduled wait.

findings.summary: Generation-task preload API returns before promised full task prefetch completes.
evidence: Inside `preloadGenerationTaskMappings`, each `prefetchQuery` is fired in `mappings.forEach` but never awaited (`generationTaskCache.ts` lines 45-63).
The function name/options (`preloadFullTaskData`) imply lifecycle completion semantics, but returned promise only guarantees mapping fetch loop completion.
Consumers relying on this seam for readiness can observe missing `taskQueryKeys.single(taskId)` cache entries immediately after await.
findings.suggestion: Collect `prefetchQuery` promises per batch and `await Promise.all` before advancing; alternatively rename the option/function to explicitly document fire-and-forget semantics.

findings.summary: Shared edge helpers emit mixed error response shapes, reducing API predictability.
evidence: `parseJsonBodyStrict` maps failures to `new Response(result.message, { status })` (plain text, no JSON envelope) via `toErrorResponse` (`edgeRequest.ts` lines 73-75, 161-163).
`rateLimitResponse` returns structured JSON body plus CORS/ratelimit headers (`rateLimit.ts` lines 216-230).
Endpoints using both helpers can return different content types and error schemas for similar client-visible failures.
findings.suggestion: Standardize shared edge error serialization (single helper returning JSON envelope + consistent headers), then route `parseJsonBodyStrict` and `rateLimitResponse` through that helper.

findings.summary: Edge storage path utilities are maintained as a manual copy, preserving migration debt and divergence risk.
evidence: supabase/functions/_shared/storagePaths.ts line 4 states it is a copy of another file and line 5 instructs manual synchronization.
The pattern mirrors static duplicated constant structures (e.g., palette arrays in src/shared/lib/segmentColors.ts lines 12-27) that can drift when edited independently.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared path contract package for both runtimes or generate the edge variant from a single source during build, then remove the manual-sync comment path.

findings.summary: Legacy bridge/shim code remains as permanent compatibility infrastructure rather than a bounded migration.
evidence: src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts lines 2-6 describe backward-compatible legacy import behavior instead of direct ownership.
supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts lines 27-33 and 89-94 keep multiple legacy parameter paths active in extract/filter logic.
findings.suggestion: Define a migration cutoff, remove deprecated input paths, and collapse bridge exports to canonical modules once callers are updated.

findings.summary: AI prompt endpoint embeds repetitive mega-prompts and duplicated guard text, increasing maintenance debt.
evidence: supabase/functions/ai-prompt/index.ts uses extensive repeated instruction strings with overlapping constraints across branches (generate_prompts/edit_prompt/enhance_segment_prompt).
supabase/functions/ai-prompt/index.ts and supabase/functions/_shared/systemLogger.ts both start with file-wide lint ignore directives, reducing pressure to keep these large implementations tidy.
findings.suggestion: Extract prompt templates into versioned constants/builders, deduplicate repeated policy clauses, and remove file-wide lint ignores in favor of targeted suppressions.

findings.summary: Timeline position hook has over-defensive local state choreography that is difficult to reason about safely.
evidence: useTimelinePositions coordinates multiple mutable refs (isLockedRef/isUpdatingRef/writeInFlightRef/pendingUpdatesRef/snapshotRef) plus delayed status reset timer, spreading invariants across many branches.
useOperationTracking also relies on timeout-based suppression (100ms window), indicating related timing-driven coordination instead of explicit transactional lifecycle boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Refactor both flows around an explicit finite-state reducer (idle/optimistic/persisting/error) with one lock source and deterministic transitions, minimizing timeout-based synchronization.

findings.summary: Request validation repeats near-identical error branches and mixed response construction styles.
evidence: update-task-status/request.ts repeats logger.error + flush + response construction for each field check (lines 40-109).
The module mixes plain Response and jsonResponse helper, while ai-prompt/index.ts consistently uses a centralized jsonResponse helper, showing seam inconsistency.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a small validation helper that emits standardized errors (log+flush+json response) and use one response strategy across edge handlers.

findings.summary: `useCredits` is placed as a shared hook but owns billing-domain orchestration and payment flow concerns.
evidence: `useCredits.ts` includes Supabase table access (`users`, `credits_ledger`), Stripe checkout invocation, admin grant-credits mutation, and navigation side effects, which are domain/service responsibilities rather than generic shared UI hook behavior.
Its test file has to mock multiple infra modules and payment flow dependencies, showing this hook sits above a simple hook-layer boundary.
findings.suggestion: Move credit/billing orchestration into a domain package (for example `src/domains/credits/` or `src/features/billing/`) with separate data-access/service modules, then keep `useCredits` as a thin adapter hook near that domain.

findings.summary: The credits hook test reflects stale or inconsistent package boundaries compared with the production module structure.
evidence: Production imports `handleError` from `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`, but test mocks `@/shared/lib/errorHandler`.
Production imports `creditQueryKeys` from `@/shared/lib/queryKeys/credits`, but test mocks `@/shared/lib/queryKeys` with a different object shape.
findings.suggestion: Align test mocks to current package entry points and consolidate query-key/error modules behind stable public paths so tests and runtime use the same boundary contracts.

findings.summary: `autoplayMonitor` resides in `shared/lib` but behaves like dev-runtime bootstrap code with automatic global side effects.
evidence: `autoplayMonitor.ts` auto-starts monitoring at module load in dev (`if (import.meta.env.DEV && typeof window !== 'undefined')` + `setTimeout`/`setInterval`).
This behavior is environment/bootstrap-oriented and not a pure reusable library pattern expected from `shared/lib`.
findings.suggestion: Relocate this module under a diagnostics/devtools/bootstrap area and require explicit initialization from app startup instead of import-time auto-execution.

findings.summary: GenerationRow compatibility policy in README no longer matches the actual facade implementation.
evidence: README claims a two-layer ownership model with compatibility aliases and a migration rule to retire alias fields.
`src/types/generationRow.ts` contains only `export type { GenerationRow } from '@/domains/generation/types/generationRow';` and no alias fields or boundary mapping logic.
findings.suggestion: Either reduce README to document the current pure re-export contract, or reintroduce an actual compatibility facade (with explicit alias mapping and deprecation plan) so policy and runtime align.

findings.summary: Supabase ownership contract is ambiguous because explicit bootstrap is documented while lazy fallback is the real runtime path.
evidence: README says client ownership is centralized and should be accessed canonically with bootstrap-oriented contract language.
`initializeSupabaseClient()` says bootstrap should call it for explicit ownership, but `getSupabaseClient()` always calls it and therefore silently initializes when bootstrap did not run.
findings.suggestion: Choose one contract and enforce it: either require explicit bootstrap (throw/assert before init in non-bootstrap contexts) or document lazy-init as first-class and remove bootstrap-required wording from governance docs.

findings.summary: Scheduled generation invalidation waits twice, doubling intended delay.
evidence: `scheduleDedupedInvalidation` sets `setTimeout(..., delayMs)` and invokes `runInvalidationLifecycle(queryClient, shotId, immediateOptions)`.
`runInvalidationLifecycle` reads `options.delayMs` and calls `await wait(delayMs)` before invalidating, so delayed calls can incur timeout delay plus internal wait delay.
The delayed test advances exactly 500ms and expects invalidation to fire, indicating intended single-delay behavior.
findings.suggestion: Use one delay mechanism only: either pass stripped options without `delayMs` into lifecycle and keep the outer timer, or remove outer timer and let lifecycle own waiting. Update delayed tests to assert exact timing with fake timers.

findings.summary: Referral finalization ignores Supabase RPC error responses and clears referral state even when finalize fails.
evidence: `useAuthReferralFinalize` calls `await supabase.rpc('create_referral_from_session', ...)` but does not destructure/check returned `error` before proceeding.
The `finally` block always removes referral storage keys, so a non-throwing RPC failure can drop retry data.
A neighboring auth hook (`useDiscordSignIn`) explicitly checks `{ error }` after Supabase call, showing expected error-handling pattern in this area.
findings.suggestion: Capture RPC result (`const { error } = await supabase.rpc(...)`), handle `error` explicitly via `handleError`, and only clear referral keys after confirmed success (or preserve keys for retry on failure).
